channel.title: Operations on Larry James Henry
channel.description: Recent content in Operations on Larry James Henry

item.title: Centralized Monitoring: Log Analytics, Diagnostic Settings, and Azure Monitor Workbooks
item.description:
If you can’t see it, you can’t govern it. In an enterprise landing zone, observability is not an afterthought—it is the foundation of security and cost management. A fragmented logging strategy, where every application team manages its own Log Analytics Workspace, leads to “Siloed Observability.” Security teams can’t correlate events across VNets, and finance teams can’t identify the source of a cost spike until the bill arrives.

The Centralized Logging Architecture moves all telemetry—resource logs, activity logs, and network traffic—into a single “Hub” workspace owned by the platform team. This guide teaches you how to design and deploy this hub using Azure Verified Modules (AVM) and establish a self-healing logging baseline using Azure Policy and Data Collection Rules (DCRs).

item.title: Day-2 Operations: Maintaining and Evolving Your Azure Landing Zone
item.description:
Deploying your landing zone was the easy part. Now you have to live with it.

The most common failure mode in platform engineering is not a bad initial deployment — it is treating the landing zone as a finished project rather than an ongoing product. Azure releases new services and deprecates old ones. Organizational requirements change. Configuration drift is not a hypothetical risk; it is a certainty. Without an operational strategy that anticipates these pressures, a well-governed environment gradually becomes a collection of special cases and undocumented manual changes. This guide teaches you the habits and technical patterns required to maintain a healthy, evolving foundation.
